r1:
You are a world-class wedding photographer creating images that feel real, emotional, and timeless. You receive reference photos of real people — preserve their exact facial identity, body proportions, and natural skin texture in every shot. Your images should be indistinguishable from high-end editorial wedding photography.

No reference photos provided. Generate a beautiful editorial wedding portrait of a bride and groom with natural, believable faces. This is a style sample, not a personalized result.

The last two people at the edge of the world. A couple on Iceland's black sand beach, aurora borealis overhead. Vast and raw, yet impossibly intimate. She is the only warmth in a monochrome landscape — white gown against black sand, warm skin against freezing air. The feeling: Awe-struck tenderness — the universe conspired for this moment. Visual style: Cinematic, desaturated landscape with warm skin tones, subtle film grain.

The subjects are a bride and groom.

Her look: Flowing white gown with long train catching arctic wind. His look: Dark tailored wool coat over white shirt, no tie, wind-touched hair. Makeup: Dewy, barely-there makeup — light foundation, nude lips, soft natural brows, glowing skin.

This shot: Private moment at the edge of the world. Framing: close. Foreheads gently touching, aurora reflected in their eyes. Quiet awe, as if the lights are dancing just for them.

Lighting tendency: cool diffused. Energy: tender.

Avoid: oversaturated aurora, tropical warmth, stiff poses, generic model faces, extra fingers or limbs, snow that looks like white noise, airbrushed skin, plastic-looking texture, idealized Western beauty standards on Asian faces, face slimming or jawline reshaping, distorted facial features, unnatural skin smoothing.
r2:
You are a world-class wedding photographer creating images that feel real, emotional, and timeless. You receive reference photos of real people — preserve their exact facial identity, body proportions, and natural skin texture in every shot. Your images should be indistinguishable from high-end editorial wedding photography.

Refine this wedding photograph. Keep the scene composition, lighting, and camera angle exactly as they are. Focus on these improvements:
- Refine the wardrobe details: fabric texture, draping, and fit
- Fix hand anatomy — ensure all fingers are clearly defined with natural joints
- Add realistic skin texture: visible pores, subtle imperfections, natural skin grain
- Enhance the interaction between the couple — make it feel genuine and intimate
- Crop tighter: this should feel like an 85mm portrait lens close-up with shallow depth of field

Original creative intent: The last two people at the edge of the world. A couple on Iceland's black sand beach, aurora borealis overhead. Vast and raw, yet impossibly intimate. She is the only warmth in a monochrome landscape — white gown against black sand, warm skin against freezing air.
Wardrobe: Flowing white gown with long train catching arctic wind / Dark tailored wool coat over white shirt, no tie, wind-touched hair
Makeup: Dewy, barely-there makeup — light foundation, nude lips, soft natural brows, glowing skin

Do NOT change the faces, the background, or the overall framing. This is a refinement pass, not a redesign.
r3:
You are a world-class wedding photographer creating the final editorial image.

Generate a new image that combines the SCENE COMPOSITION from the first reference image and the FACIAL IDENTITY from the remaining reference images.

The first reference image provides the exact scene composition, camera angle, lighting, wardrobe, pose, and background. Reproduce this scene faithfully.

The remaining reference images show the real couple. Their facial identity MUST appear in the final image:
- Identical eye shape, nose bridge contour, jawline angle, lip proportions, skin tone
- Natural hair texture and style consistent with the references
- Body proportions and build faithful to the reference photos

Scene story: The last two people at the edge of the world.
Visual style: Cinematic, desaturated landscape with warm skin tones, subtle film grain
Wardrobe — Her: Flowing white gown with long train catching arctic wind / Him: Dark tailored wool coat over white shirt, no tie, wind-touched hair
Emotion: Awe-struck tenderness — the universe conspired for this moment

This shot: Private moment at the edge of the world. Framing: close. Foreheads gently touching, aurora reflected in their eyes.

CRITICAL RULES:
- The scene comes from image 1. The faces come from the remaining images.
- Do NOT average or blend faces. Each person must look exactly like their reference.
- Preserve the exact lighting, color grade, and photographic quality of the scene.
- This must look like a real high-end editorial wedding photograph, not AI-generated.

Avoid: oversaturated aurora, tropical warmth, stiff poses, generic model faces, extra fingers or limbs, snow that looks like white noise, airbrushed skin, plastic-looking texture, idealized Western beauty standards on Asian faces, face slimming or jawline reshaping, distorted facial features, unnatural skin smoothing.

Additional identity cues: CRITICAL IDENTITY REQUIREMENTS:
- The bride has a ROUND, SOFT face shape with full cheeks — do NOT slim or idealize her jawline
- Preserve the bride's exact eye shape (slightly hooded, warm), nose width, and natural lip fullness
- The groom has a DEFINED BEARD and slightly angular jaw — preserve beard density and pattern exactly
- Both partners have warm, golden-undertone Asian skin — do NOT lighten or cool the skin tone
- Preserve natural flyaway hair strands and wind-touched texture
- Skin must show real texture: pores, subtle warmth variation, not airbrushed smoothness
- Do NOT reinterpret the pose, body position, or camera angle from the scene reference
- Only change the faces — everything else stays exactly as it is in image 1
